title: SENIOR HEALTH INSURANCE BENEFITS ASSISTANCE - 211info
text: COUNCIL ON AGING OF CENTRAL OREGON SENIOR HEALTH INSURANCE BENEFITS ASSISTANCE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides trained volunteers who educate, assist, and serve as advocates for people with Medicare. SHIBA volunteers help people with Medicare understand their rights and options for health insurance so that they can make informed decisions. SHIBA volunteers can also provide information on the Qualified Medicare Beneficiary (QMB) program, a Medicaid program that helps people with Medicare pay for their medical care. 1036 NE 5th Street Bend, OR 97701 Eligibility: Individuals with Medicare and their family members or caregivers Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-4:30pm Email (541) 678-5483 Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers Main phone (541) 678-5483 Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: Call or visit website for more information FEES: None DOCUMENTS: None required at intake AREA SERVED: Crook, Deschutes, and Jefferson Counties SITE HOURS: Monday-Friday 8am-4:30pm Services: Health Insurance Claims Assistance for Beneficiaries * Medicare Beneficiaries Medicare Information/Counseling Other Services Offered At This Location: COMMUNITY GROUPS AND GOVERNMENT/ADMINISTRATIVE OFFICES - AREA AGENCY ON AGING DOMESTIC ANIMAL SERVICES - PET PALS FOOD - MEALS ON WHEELS HOME-DELIVERED MEALS HEALTH SUPPORTIVE SERVICES - AGING AND DISABILITY RESOURCE CONNECTION INDIVIDUAL AND FAMILY SUPPORT SERVICES - CARING CONNECTIONS INDIVIDUAL AND FAMILY SUPPORT SERVICES - FAMILY CAREGIVER SUPPORT PROGRAM INDIVIDUAL AND FAMILY SUPPORT SERVICES - OPTIONS COUNSELING INDIVIDUAL AND FAMILY SUPPORT SERVICES - OREGON PROJECT INDEPENDENCE If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service. COUNCIL ON AGING OF CENTRAL OREGON
